topic: Why AI-assisted teams still fail on decision quality

candidate_explanations.title: Tool Adoption Is Enough
candidate_explanations.claim: Teams improve decision quality if they adopt more advanced AI tools because better tools create better decisions.
candidate_explanations.mechanism: More tool adoption increases output speed and therefore decision quality.
assumptions: Output speed is a proxy for decision quality
Teams use tools correctly by default

evidence.claim: Teams report productivity gains after AI deployment

candidate_explanations.title: Evaluation Bottleneck Hypothesis
candidate_explanations.claim: AI tools increase option generation, but decision quality improves only when teams upgrade evaluation protocols and accountability loops.
candidate_explanations.mechanism: AI expands candidate actions; explicit evaluation criteria, post-decision review, and error ownership reduce false positives and calibration drift over time.
candidate_explanations.scope: Cross-functional knowledge teams in product, policy, and operations.
assumptions: Teams can run regular post-decision reviews

evidence.claim: Model-assisted workflows vary by human evaluation protocol
Governance and measurement loops are needed for reliable AI use
Prompt quality without review loops leads to brittle outcomes

predictions.statement: Teams with explicit evaluation rubrics will reduce avoidable decision reversals.
Teams with monthly retrospective scoring will improve forecast calibration.

falsifiers.condition: Evaluation-protocol teams do not outperform output-only teams on decision quality
falsifiers.test_method: controlled A/B comparison across matched teams

falsifiers.condition: Calibration metrics stay flat despite accountability loop adoption
falsifiers.test_method: pre/post calibration audit with fixed scoring rubric

candidate_explanations.title: Culture-Only Explanation
candidate_explanations.claim: Decision quality depends entirely on company culture and leadership mindset.
candidate_explanations.mechanism: Strong culture motivates better decisions in all contexts.
